description: Running CPU-bound or blocking std calls directly on a tokio worker thread, starving other tasks on that thread.
body: Failure mode: a tokio task does heavy computation or std::fs/std::net blocking IO inline, monopolizing a runtime worker so other tasks scheduled on it cannot progress and latency spikes. Detection signals: tokio-console shows tasks with long poll times; throughput drops as load rises; a thread is pinned at 100% while others idle. Do not block a tokio worker thread. Safer fallback: move blocking/CPU work to tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or a dedicated rayon pool) and await its handle, then verify worker poll times stay short under load.
